name: Big O
description: Questions on Big O notation and asymptotic behavior.

questions.question: Big O describes:
choices: Upper bound on growth as input grows
questions.explanation: Big O is an asymptotic upper bound: growth rate in the limit.

questions.question: Which grows faster asymptotically?
questions.explanation: As n grows, n grows faster than log n; O(n) dominates O(log n).

questions.question: O(n log n) is typical for:
questions.explanation: Optimal comparison-based sorting is Theta(n log n).

questions.question: We say algorithm A is O(n^2). That means:
choices: It always runs in exactly n^2 steps
For large n, runtime is bounded above by a constant times n^2
questions.explanation: O(n^2) means runtime <= c*n^2 for some constant c and large n.
